skills.name: agent-skills
skills.summary: Scans the repository skill folders, excludes planning skills, and generates a markdown inventory with backlinks to each non-plan skill file. It standardizes skill discovery for reference and navigation workflows.
primary_use_cases: Generate an index of non-plan skills
Document available reusable agent skills
Create backlink-based skill reference pages

skills.name: creating-dynamic-wireframes
skills.summary: Reads PRDs or related product documents and converts them into deterministic low-fidelity wireframes with states, triggers, navigation, and ASCII layouts. It supports interpretation, default-file, and update workflows.
primary_use_cases: Generate wireframes from a PRD
Map page states before UI implementation
Create structured ASCII page layouts for MVP planning

skills.name: debugging-wizard
skills.summary: Applies a structured debug workflow that reproduces issues, isolates failures, tests hypotheses, and verifies fixes with prevention steps. It also provides language-specific debugging references and command patterns.
primary_use_cases: Investigate runtime errors and crashes
Trace stack traces to isolate failure points
Add regression coverage after fixing a bug

skills.name: defining-tech-stack
skills.summary: Derives a production-ready stack definition from an MVP PRD and frontend blueprint by resolving architectural ambiguity one question at a time. It outputs a single authoritative `stack-definition.md` for downstream research and implementation.
primary_use_cases: Select backend technologies for an MVP
Align database, auth, hosting, and testing choices
Produce a canonical stack-definition document

skills.name: designing-ui-ux-prd
skills.summary: Transforms draft PRDs into an implementation-ready UI/UX PRD with page layouts, navigation, buttons, glyph suggestions, and interaction rules. It enforces deterministic visual-direction decisions through one-at-a-time UX clarification.
primary_use_cases: Create a UI/UX PRD from a product PRD
Define MVP page inventory and navigation structure
Specify buttons, glyphs, and interaction behavior before implementation

skills.name: dynamic-wireframe-mapper
skills.summary: Analyzes PRDs, UI drafts, or user-flow documents and generates ASCII dependency graphs that connect user actions, UI components, state changes, and backend responses. It adapts graph structure dynamically to the source document type.
primary_use_cases: Visualize component dependencies from documentation
Map UI-to-backend interaction chains
Create ASCII system flow diagrams from PRDs or user flows

skills.name: git-worktree-orchestrator
skills.summary: Creates isolated git worktrees for feature execution, including directory selection, safety checks, environment setup, and baseline test validation. It enforces deterministic setup before implementation work begins.
primary_use_cases: Start new feature work in an isolated worktree
Prepare a clean environment for implementation plans
Validate baseline project health before coding

skills.name: mapping-user-flow-progression
skills.summary: Maps detailed page progression, trigger-level interactions, state transitions, and backend hooks into reproducible UX flow documents. It writes both a master overview and per-page flow artifacts.
primary_use_cases: Define click-level UX progression before implementation
Document page entry and exit behavior
Generate deterministic trigger maps for frontend flows

skills.name: plan-draft-all-core
skills.summary: Supervises the full Stage 1 through Stage 4 planning pipeline by invoking each stage in order and stopping on first failure. It produces a consolidated report and preserves stage-owned boundaries.
primary_use_cases: Run the full multi-stage planning pipeline
Coordinate stage drafting in strict order
Generate a consolidated planning progress report

skills.name: plan-freeze-stage1-core
skills.summary: Validates and freezes Stage 1 planning artifacts by reconciling scope, stack, gates, and architecture decisions. It writes a freeze review and updates manifest and state to a Stage 1 frozen state.
primary_use_cases: Freeze Stage 1 planning outputs
Validate Stage 1 readiness before Stage 2
Produce a Stage 1 freeze review artifact

skills.name: plan-freeze-stage2-core
skills.summary: Reconciles Stage 2 contract artifacts, validates readiness, and transitions the planning workspace to a Stage 2 frozen state. It focuses on data model, contract, integration, and security consistency.
primary_use_cases: Freeze Stage 2 planning artifacts
Validate backend contract consistency before Stage 3
Create a Stage 2 freeze review report

skills.name: plan-freeze-stage3-core
skills.summary: Freezes Stage 3 by validating workflow, frontend experience, component strategy, and analytics assumptions against the approved template set. It records the result in paired review artifacts and metadata.
primary_use_cases: Freeze Stage 3 UX and component planning
Validate frontend planning consistency before Stage 4
Generate a Stage 3 freeze review

skills.name: plan-freeze-stage4-core
skills.summary: Freezes Stage 4 delivery planning by reconciling infrastructure, QA, operations, risk, and release-slice assumptions. It writes the freeze review and updates planning metadata to Stage 4 frozen.
primary_use_cases: Freeze Stage 4 delivery artifacts
Validate release readiness planning
Produce a final Stage 4 freeze review

skills.name: plan-reopen-stage-core
skills.summary: Reopens a previously frozen planning stage, records the reason, and invalidates downstream frozen stages when necessary. It restores a valid state-machine path through paired reopen reports and metadata changes.
primary_use_cases: Reopen a frozen planning stage for revisions
Invalidate downstream stages after upstream changes
Generate a stage-reopen audit report

skills.name: plan-slices-core
skills.summary: Generates slice implementation plans from frozen Stage 1 through Stage 4 planning outputs and selected planning templates. It preserves slice ordering constraints and updates the planning workspace state.
primary_use_cases: Create implementation slices from frozen plans
Translate planning outputs into slice-by-slice execution artifacts
Set slice ordering based on roadmap constraints

skills.name: plan-stage1-core
skills.summary: Initial Stage 1 planning worker that reads intake and PRD artifacts, generates bounded Stage 1 plans, and updates metadata without freezing the stage. It establishes MVP scope, architecture direction, and dependency gates.
primary_use_cases: Draft Stage 1 planning artifacts
Generate MVP scope and architecture direction
Prepare a planning workspace for Stage 1 review

skills.name: plan-stage2-core
skills.summary: Drafts Stage 2 contract and constraint artifacts using frozen Stage 1 context plus any activated conditional templates. It updates metadata while keeping work strictly inside Stage 2 boundaries.
primary_use_cases: Draft Stage 2 contracts and data models
Generate API, runtime, and schema planning artifacts
Advance a planning workspace into Stage 2 drafting

skills.name: plan-stage3-core
skills.summary: Drafts Stage 3 workflow and frontend artifacts from frozen Stage 2 context, including UX, component system, and optional analytics plans. It updates manifest and state while keeping the work unfrozen and Stage 3-scoped.
primary_use_cases: Draft Stage 3 UX and frontend planning artifacts
Generate component system and interaction plans
Advance planning into frontend workflow definition

skills.name: plan-stage4-core
skills.summary: Drafts Stage 4 delivery, release-readiness, infrastructure, and risk artifacts from frozen Stage 3 context. It prepares the planning workspace for final operational review without freezing the stage.
primary_use_cases: Draft Stage 4 delivery planning artifacts
Generate deployment and QA readiness plans
Prepare release-slice and operations planning

skills.name: plan-start-core
skills.summary: Initializes the planning workspace for a new app idea by drafting intake and PRD artifacts, selecting the planning path, and creating manifest and state files. It is the foundational entry point for the multi-stage planning system.
primary_use_cases: Start a new planning workspace for an app idea
Generate intake and PRD artifacts from normalized inputs
Initialize manifest and state for downstream planning commands

skills.name: plan-status-core
skills.summary: Inspects planning manifest and state files to produce a paired status report for the current planning workspace. It surfaces current stage, blockers, frozen stages, active templates, and next valid commands.
primary_use_cases: Check current planning workspace status
Report blockers and valid next commands
Generate a machine-readable planning status artifact

skills.name: plan-tasks-core
skills.summary: Generates one task list per slice from slice implementation plans and updates the planning manifest and state. It keeps task ordering grounded in the exact frozen planning documents and orchestration templates.
primary_use_cases: Translate slice plans into task lists
Generate execution-ready task artifacts per slice
Advance planning state to task list generation

skills.name: plan-tickets-core
skills.summary: Converts slice task lists into execution-ready implementation tickets while preserving slice grouping and stage ownership. It writes paired markdown and JSON ticket artifacts and updates planning state.
primary_use_cases: Generate implementation tickets from task lists
Prepare execution artifacts for downstream builders
Advance planning state to ticket generation

skills.name: researching-tech-stack-best-practices
skills.summary: Reads a confirmed `stack-definition.md`, researches each declared technology independently via an MCP source such as Context7, and writes modular best-practice guides plus a stack overview. It treats each technology as a separate research unit and validates output coverage before completion.
primary_use_cases: Research production best practices for a chosen stack
Generate one best-practice file per technology
Produce a cross-stack architecture overview

skills.name: security-review
skills.summary: Provides a security-first review framework for authentication, input handling, secrets, APIs, payments, and sensitive data flows. It combines implementation patterns, checklists, and pre-deployment validation criteria to reduce common vulnerabilities.
primary_use_cases: Review new auth or authorization code
Validate input handling and API security
Run a pre-deployment security checklist

skills.name: swift-expert
skills.summary: Specialist implementation skill for Swift ecosystems covering SwiftUI, protocol-oriented architecture, async/await concurrency, actors, performance, and testing. It provides concrete code patterns and validation checkpoints for Apple-platform and server-side Swift work.
primary_use_cases: Implement SwiftUI views and view models
Design protocol-oriented Swift architectures
Build and test async Swift concurrency code

skills.name: systematic-debugger
skills.summary: Enforces a strict root-cause-first debugging process that forbids fixes before evidence gathering, pattern analysis, and minimal hypothesis testing. It is designed to stop guess-and-check debugging and drive reliable bug resolution.
primary_use_cases: Investigate bugs before proposing fixes
Analyze repeated failed fix attempts
Create a root-cause-driven path to verified remediation

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.summary: Imposes strict test-first implementation for features, bug fixes, and refactors using the red-green-refactor cycle. It requires watching a test fail before writing production code and validating green status before cleanup.
primary_use_cases: Implement a new feature with test-first discipline
Fix a bug by reproducing it with a failing test
Refactor safely with behavior locked by tests

skills.name: worktree-merge-manager
skills.summary: Safely merges or discards a git worktree using a human-in-the-loop confirmation flow, clean-state validation, and post-action cleanup. It is specifically designed to prevent accidental data loss during worktree lifecycle completion.
primary_use_cases: Merge a completed worktree branch into its base branch
Discard an abandoned worktree safely
Clean up worktrees after feature isolation
